sourceMovie.title: The Star
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Star (2017) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Shrek
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Star clicked for you, Shrek (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: DuckTales: The Movie - Treasure of the Lost Lamp
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Star clicked for you, DuckTales: The Movie - Treasure of the Lost Lamp (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Star?
faq.answer: Try Shrek, DuckTales: The Movie - Treasure of the Lost Lamp, Shrek the Halls—each shares DNA with The Star while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Star?
faq.answer: The Star (2017) is best described as Animation, Comedy, Adventure, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Star worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
